meaning: vi.瑙ｈ劚,瑙ｅ紑绾犵粨,鏉惧紑,瑙ｅ喅(绾犵悍)vt.瑙ｅ紑,鏉惧紑
examples: She clawed at the bushes to disentangle herself.
He tried to disentangle his fingers from her hair.
It's impossible to disentangle the myth from reality.
The effect of Mr Donaldson's tenure is hard to disentangle from that of Mr Spitzer's.
It is hard to disentangle diversification from desertion since each feeds on the other.
